content: Antibiotic resistance is a big problem that's happening all around the world, and it's making a lot of people sick. When we take antibiotics, they help kill the bad germs that make us sick, but sometimes those germs can become super strong and the antibiotics don't work anymore. One reason this is happening is that people are taking antibiotics too much, even when they're not really needed, like when they have a cold or the flu. When we take antibiotics too much, it helps the strong germs grow and multiply, making them even stronger and harder to kill. Lots of people all around the world are taking antibiotics too much, and that's making the problem of antibiotic resistance spread really fast. We can all help by only taking antibiotics when we really need them, and by making sure to finish the whole dose like our doctor tells us to. Doctors and scientists are working really hard to find new ways to kill the super strong germs and to make new antibiotics that will work. If we all do our part and are careful with antibiotics, we can help slow down the spread of antibiotic resistance and keep people from getting really sick.
